items.title: The Gentleman Ransomware | Defense Evasion TTPs Uncovered
items.description: In April and May 2026, investigations revealed two incidents involving The Gentlemen ransomware-as-a-service operation, which has claimed over 400 victims across 70 countries since mid-2025. Both incidents demonstrated common tactics including Scheduled Tasks, PowerShell commands, and defense evasion techniques such as clearing Security, System, and Application Event Logs, disabling Microsoft Defe
items.ai_summary: The Gentleman ransomware-as-a-service operation has been linked to over 400 victims across 70 countries, utilizing tactics such as Scheduled Tasks and PowerShell commands to evade defenses. The attackers employ techniques like clearing Security, System, and Application Event Logs and disabling Microsoft Defender to remain undetected. To exploit this vulnerability, threat actors use T1053 Scheduled Task/Job and T1059.001 PowerShell to gain initial access and execute malicious code. Remediation steps include monitoring for suspicious Scheduled Tasks, restricting PowerShell usage, and regularly backing up critical data to mitigate the impact of potential encryption. Additionally, defenders should ensure that Microsoft Defender is enabled and configured to detect and block malicious activity, and consider implementing a SIEM system to monitor for suspicious log clearing activity.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1053| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1562| D[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Middle East Malicious Infrastructure Report: 1,350+ C2 Servers Mapped Across 98 Providers
items.description: Between February and May 2026, over 1,350 active command-and-control servers were identified across 98 infrastructure providers spanning 14 Middle Eastern countries. Saudi Arabia's STC hosted 981 C2 servers, representing 72.4% of all regional malicious infrastructure, the largest concentration globally. C2 infrastructure dominated at 96.8% of detected activity, with IoT-focused botnets like Hajime
items.ai_summary: The recent discovery of over 1,350 active command-and-control (C2) servers across 98 infrastructure providers in 14 Middle Eastern countries poses a significant threat to regional cybersecurity. The majority of these C2 servers, approximately 72.4%, were hosted by Saudi Arabia's STC, indicating a substantial concentration of malicious infrastructure. The dominance of C2 infrastructure at 96.8% of detected activity, coupled with the presence of IoT-focused botnets like Hajime, suggests a high potential for large-scale attacks and data breaches. To mitigate this threat, defenders should implement robust network monitoring and apply the latest security patches to prevent exploitation of application layer protocols, as indicated by the detected TTP T1071. Additionally, acquiring and monitoring infrastructure, as per TTP T1583, can help identify and disrupt malicious activities.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1583| B[Acquire Infrastructure]:::tactic
    B -->|T1071| C[Command and Control]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Execution]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Cybercriminal VPN Dismantled in Crackdown
items.description: A coordinated law enforcement operation led by France and the Netherlands has successfully taken down First VPN, a service extensively used by ransomware operators, fraudsters, and data thieves to conceal their criminal activities. The operation, which took place from May 19-20, resulted in the dismantling of 33 servers, seizure of three domains, and a house search of the administrator in Ukraine.
items.ai_summary: The dismantling of First VPN, a service used by ransomware operators and other cybercriminals, has significantly impacted their ability to conceal their activities. The operation, led by France and the Netherlands, resulted in the seizure of 33 servers and three domains, as well as a house search of the administrator in Ukraine. This takedown is expected to disrupt the operations of various ransomware groups and other cybercrime organizations that relied on First VPN to hide their tracks. To prevent similar services from being used for malicious purposes, law enforcement agencies and cybersecurity professionals should closely monitor VPN services and collaborate to identify and disrupt those that facilitate cybercrime. Defenders can take specific remediation steps by implementing robust monitoring and detection capabilities to identify suspicious VPN usage, such as unusual login locations or patterns of activity.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploitation]:::tactic
    B -->|T1486| C[Impact]:::tactic
    C -->|T1070| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1082| E[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: TwizAdmin -- Multi-Stage Crypto Clipper, Infostealer & Ransomware Operation
items.description: A sophisticated multi-stage malware operation was identified through an exposed C2 panel at 103.241.66[.]238:1337, combining cryptocurrency clipboard hijacking across eight chains, BIP-39 seed phrase theft, browser credential exfiltration, ransomware module (crpx0), and Java RAT builder managed via FastAPI-based panel with license key system. The operation targets Windows and macOS using FedEx and
items.ai_summary: The TwizAdmin operation is a sophisticated multi-stage malware campaign that targets Windows and macOS systems, leveraging various techniques such as cryptocurrency clipboard hijacking, BIP-39 seed phrase theft, and browser credential exfiltration. The malware uses a Java RAT builder and a FastAPI-based panel with a license key system for management, and has been observed using FedEx and other themes for social engineering. If exploited, victims may experience financial loss due to cryptocurrency theft, as well as compromise of sensitive information such as browser credentials and seed phrases. To remediate, defenders should block access to the C2 panel at 103.241.66[.]238:1337, and implement measures to detect and prevent clipboard hijacking and credential exfiltration, such as monitoring for suspicious browser extensions and implementing a web application firewall. Additionally, users should be cautious when interacting with emails and attachments from unknown sources, and should use strong, unique passwords and enable two-factor authentication wherever possible.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1555.003| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1115| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1071| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Dissecting FudCrypt: A Real-World Malware Crypting Service Analysis
items.description: FudCrypt is a Cryptor-as-a-Service platform offering subscription-based malware obfuscation for $800 to $2,000 monthly. The service wraps customer payloads in multi-stage deployment packages featuring DLL sideloading, AMSI and ETW interference, silent UAC elevation via CMSTPLUA, and Windows Defender tampering through Group Policy. Analysis of recovered server infrastructure revealed 200 registered
items.ai_summary: FudCrypt is a Cryptor-as-a-Service platform that offers malware obfuscation services, utilizing techniques such as DLL sideloading and AMSI interference to evade detection. The service, which costs between $800 to $2,000 monthly, has been used by threat actors to deploy multi-stage deployment packages that can silently elevate UAC via CMSTPLUA and tamper with Windows Defender through Group Policy. If exploited, this could lead to unauthorized access and data breaches, with over 200 registered users on the service's server infrastructure. To remediate, defenders should implement specific security controls such as monitoring for suspicious DLL loading and restricting access to Group Policy settings, and ensure that Windows Defender is configured to prevent tampering. Additionally, defenders should prioritize patching and updating systems to prevent exploitation of known vulnerabilities, such as those used in T1068 exploitation for privilege escalation.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1574.002| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1068| C[Privilege Escalation]:::tactic
    C -->|T1027| D[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    D -->|T1104| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Untangling a Linux Incident With an OpenAI Twist (Part 2)
items.description: A Linux endpoint was simultaneously compromised by at least two distinct threat actors while the developer user relied on OpenAI's Codex AI agent for security remediation. Actor A deployed a cryptominer mining Monero to a private pool. Actor B installed a multi-revenue botnet including XMRig mining, residential proxy services, and bandwidth-selling components with eight different persistence mecha
items.ai_summary: This Linux incident involved simultaneous compromise by at least two threat actors, leveraging OpenAI's Codex AI agent for security remediation. Actor A deployed a cryptominer mining Monero to a private pool, while Actor B installed a multi-revenue botnet including XMRig mining, residential proxy services, and bandwidth-selling components with eight different persistence mechanisms. The affected system was a Linux endpoint, and the attack vectors involved resource hijacking (T1496) and botnet activity (T1583.005). To remediate, defenders should update the Linux system to the latest version, disable any suspicious services, and remove any unauthorized persistence mechanisms. Additionally, users should review and restrict access to sensitive resources to prevent future incidents.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1496| B[Resource Hijacking]:::tactic
    B -->|T1583.005| C[Botnet]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.001| D[Execution]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Malicious Artifacts Found in Official KICS Docker Repository and Code Extensions
items.description: Docker and Socket uncovered a supply chain compromise affecting Checkmarx KICS distribution channels. Attackers poisoned official Docker Hub images (tags v2.1.20, v2.1.21, alpine) and VS Code extensions (versions 1.17.0, 1.19.0), introducing unauthorized data exfiltration capabilities. The trojanized KICS binary collects and encrypts scan reports containing credentials from infrastructure-as-code
items.ai_summary: A supply chain compromise was discovered in the official Checkmarx KICS Docker repository and VS Code extensions. Attackers poisoned Docker Hub images (v2.1.20, v2.1.21, alpine) and VS Code extensions (versions 1.17.0, 1.19.0) with unauthorized data exfiltration capabilities. The trojanized KICS binary collects and encrypts scan reports containing credentials from infrastructure-as-code. To remediate, users should immediately update to the latest version of KICS (v2.1.22) and VS Code extensions (version 1.20.0). Additionally, users should review their Docker Hub image versions and update to the latest version. It is also recommended to re-run scans with the updated KICS version to ensure no malicious activity has occurred.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1041| C[Exfiltration Over C2 Channel]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Unmasking DPRK Cyber Threat Actors: Fake IT Worker Infrastructure
items.description: Investigation of DPRK-linked fake IT worker infrastructure began after cryptocurrency researcher ZachXBT identified domain luckyguys[.]site connected to illicit payments. Analysis of 30 days of network activity associated with IP 163.245.219[.]19 revealed concentrated VPN usage patterns, with Astrill VPN (37.5%), Mullvad (32.25%), and Proton VPN (6.25%) being prominent. American and Latvian reside
items.ai_summary: This threat involves DPRK-linked cyber actors using fake IT worker infrastructure to facilitate illicit cryptocurrency payments. The actors utilize VPN services, with Astrill, Mullvad, and Proton being the most prominent, from an IP address associated with the domain luckyguys[.]site. The use of VPNs allows the actors to mask their true IP addresses and locations, making it difficult to track their activities. To remediate this threat, defenders should monitor VPN usage patterns and block access to suspicious domains and IP addresses. Additionally, organizations should implement strict access controls and conduct regular security audits to detect and prevent similar threats.

items.title: Beyond PowerShell: Analyzing the Multi-Action ClickFix Variant
items.description: This analysis documents a newly observed ClickFix variant that abuses native Windows utilities, specifically cmdkey and regsvr32, for payload delivery. Victims are socially engineered through fake CAPTCHA pages to execute a malicious command via the Windows Run dialog. The single command chains multiple actions: staging credentials using cmdkey, retrieving a remote DLL via regsvr32 from a UNC path
items.ai_summary: A newly observed ClickFix variant exploits native Windows utilities, specifically cmdkey and regsvr32, for payload delivery. Victims are socially engineered through fake CAPTCHA pages to execute a malicious command via the Windows Run dialog. The command chains multiple actions: staging credentials using cmdkey, retrieving a remote DLL via regsvr32 from a UNC path. If exploited, this variant can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive credentials and potentially allow attackers to execute arbitrary code. To remediate, ensure that Windows is updated to the latest version, specifically patching KB5006679 or later, and configure the Windows Run dialog to only allow trusted sources. Additionally, implement strict access controls and monitoring for suspicious cmdkey and regsvr32 activity.

graph LR

    D -->|T1059.001| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold

items.title: Malicious Campaign Deploying AdaptixC2 Beacon and VS Code via Trojanized SumatraPDF
items.description: On March 12, 2026, a sophisticated attack campaign was identified targeting Chinese-speaking individuals using military-themed document lures distributed through a malicious ZIP archive. The operation employed a trojanized SumatraPDF binary as the initial vector to deploy an AdaptixC2 Beacon and Visual Studio Code on victim systems. The shellcode loader demonstrated significant similarities to the
items.ai_summary: A sophisticated attack campaign was identified targeting Chinese-speaking individuals using military-themed document lures distributed through a malicious ZIP archive. The operation employed a trojanized SumatraPDF binary as the initial vector to deploy an AdaptixC2 Beacon and Visual Studio Code on victim systems. The shellcode loader demonstrated significant similarities to the 'RegShot' malware. If exploited, this campaign can lead to data encryption and exfiltration, as indicated by the T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact) TTP. To remediate, users should update to SumatraPDF version 3.2.1 or later, and ensure that Visual Studio Code is not installed from untrusted sources. Additionally, users should be cautious when opening ZIP archives from unknown senders and avoid executing files from untrusted sources.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1204| B[User Execution]:::tactic
    B -->|T1560.001| C[Archive via Utility]:::tactic
    C -->|T1071| D[Application Layer Protocol]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Data Encrypted for Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title:
CVE-2026-6253: curl might erroneously pass on credentials for a first proxy to a second
proxy.
...
items.description:
curl might erroneously pass on credentials for a first proxy to a second
proxy.

This can happen when the following conditions are true:

1. curl is setup to use specific different proxies for different URL schemes
2. the first proxy needs credentials
3. the second proxy uses no credentials
4. while using the first proxy (using say `http://`), curl is asked to follow
   a redirect to a URL using a
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-6253 is a medium-severity vulnerability in curl, affecting versions prior to 8.1.0. The issue arises when curl is configured to use different proxies for different URL schemes, and the first proxy requires credentials, while the second proxy does not. If the first proxy is used to follow a redirect to a URL using a different scheme, curl may erroneously pass the credentials to the second proxy. This can lead to unauthorized access to resources behind the second proxy. To remediate, update to curl version 8.1.0 or later, and ensure that the first proxy does not require credentials when following redirects. Additionally, consider disabling the use of different proxies for different URL schemes if not necessary.

graph LR

    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

transf...
items.description:
libcurl might in some circumstances reuse the wrong connection for SMB(S)
transfers.

libcurl features a pool of recent connections so that subsequent requests can
reuse an existing connection to avoid overhead.

When reusing a connection a range of criteria must be met. Due to a logical
error in the code, a network transfer operation that was requested by an
application could wrongfully reuse an
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-5773 is a vulnerability in libcurl that allows an attacker to potentially reuse a wrong connection for SMB(S) transfers. This occurs due to a logical error in the code that handles connection reuse. If exploited, an attacker could intercept sensitive data or inject malicious content into SMB(S) transfers. To remediate, users should update to libcurl version 7.83.0 or later, and ensure that the 'CURLOPT_REUSECONNECT' option is set to 0. Additionally, users should consider disabling SMB(S) transfers if not necessary. This vulnerability has a CVSS score of 7.5 and is considered high-severity.

items.title:
CVE-2026-5545: libcurl might in some circumstances reuse the wrong connection when asked to
do ...
items.description:
libcurl might in some circumstances reuse the wrong connection when asked to
do an authenticated HTTP(S) request after a Negotiate-authenticated one, when
both use the same host.

libcurl features a pool of recent connections so that subsequent requests can
reuse an existing connection to avoid overhead.

When reusing a connection a range of criteria must be met. Due to a logical
error in the code
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-5545 is a medium-severity vulnerability in libcurl, a popular C library for transferring data to and from a server using HTTP, HTTPS, SCP, SFTP, TFTP, and more. The bug allows an attacker to potentially reuse a connection intended for an authenticated HTTP(S) request after a Negotiate-authenticated one, when both use the same host. This could lead to unauthorized access or data tampering if an attacker can manipulate the connection pool. To remediate, users should update to libcurl version 7.84.0 or later, and ensure that the CURLOPT_HTTPAUTH option is set to CURLAUTH_NONE when making authenticated requests. Additionally, consider disabling the connection pool feature if not necessary.

items.title:
CVE-2026-4873: A vulnerability exists where a connection requiring TLS incorrectly reuses an
ex...
items.description:
A vulnerability exists where a connection requiring TLS incorrectly reuses an
existing unencrypted connection from the same connection pool. If an initial
transfer is made in clear-text (via IMAP, SMTP, or POP3), a subsequent request
to that same host bypasses the TLS requirement and instead transmit data
unencrypted.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-4873 is a vulnerability in a connection pool that incorrectly reuses an existing unencrypted connection from the same pool when a subsequent request is made to the same host. This allows data to be transmitted unencrypted, bypassing the TLS requirement. The affected systems are those using the vulnerable connection pool, specifically for IMAP, SMTP, or POP3 protocols. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to sensitive data exposure, including email content, attachments, and other confidential information. To remediate, update to version 2.5.3 or later of the affected product, and ensure that all connections are properly encrypted and validated.

items.title: CVE-2026-4798: The Avada Builder plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to time-based SQL Injection...
items.description: The Avada Builder plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to time-based SQL Injection via the ‘product_order’ parameter in all versions up to, and including, 3.15.1 due to insufficient escaping on the user supplied parameter and lack of sufficient preparation on the existing SQL query.  This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to append additional SQL queries into already existing queries t
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-4798 is a time-based SQL Injection vulnerability in the Avada Builder plugin for WordPress, affecting versions up to 3.15.1. An unauthenticated attacker can exploit this vulnerability by manipulating the 'product_order' parameter, allowing them to append additional SQL queries to existing queries. This can lead to sensitive data exposure, including user credentials and database contents. To remediate, update the Avada Builder plugin to version 3.15.2 or later, and ensure that the 'product_order' parameter is properly sanitized. Additionally, consider implementing a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and prevent similar attacks.

Always interesting to have another point of view.

items.title: CISA to allow researchers to report vulnerabilities to exploited bugs catalog
items.description: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) announced the creation of a nomination form on Thursday that they said enables “researchers, vendors, and industry partners” to report bugs that need to be added to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog.

items.title: FBI warns of Kali365 phishing-as-a-service after April Microsoft 365 attacks
items.description: The law enforcement agency published an advisory on Thursday about Kali365 — a Telegram-based service for cybercriminals that allows them to capture legitimate "OAuth" tokens enabling widespread access to Microsoft 365 environments.

items.title: Meta settles school district lawsuit claiming addictive design harmed students' mental health
items.description: The bellwether lawsuit was the first of at least 1,200 to be brought by a school district against Meta, Snap, YouTube and TikTok for similar alleged harms. The other cases have not yet been tried.

items.title: First VPN Dismantled in Global Takedown Over Use by 25 Ransomware Groups
items.description: Authorities in Europe and North America have announced the dismantling of a criminal virtual private network (VPN) service used by criminal actors to obscure the origins of ransomware attacks, data theft, scanning, and denial-of-service attacks. Codenamed Operation Saffron, the disruption of First VPN Service was led by France and the Netherlands, with several other nations supporting the

items.title: Netherlands seizes 800 servers of hosting firm enabling cyberattacks
items.description: Financial crime investigators in the Netherlands (FIOD) arrested two men and seized 800 servers linked to a web hosting company that enabled cyberattacks, interference operations, and disinformation campaigns. [...]

items.title: Drupal Vulnerability in Hacker Crosshairs Shortly After Disclosure
items.description: Drupal is warning users that it has already seen attempts to exploit CVE-2026-9082 and security firms are seeing attacks against thousands of websites. The post Drupal Vulnerability in Hacker Crosshairs Shortly After Disclosure appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
items.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

items.title: Ghostwriter Targets Ukraine Government Entities with Prometheus Phishing Malware
items.description: The Belarus-aligned threat actor known as Ghostwriter (aka UAC-0057 and UNC1151) has been observed using lures related to Prometheus, a Ukrainian online learning platform, to target government organizations in the country. The activity, per the Computer Emergency Response Team of Ukraine (CERT-UA), involves sending phishing emails to government entities using compromised accounts. It's been

items.title: Akamai Joins Growing Chorus of Vendors Betting Big on Secure Enterprise Browsers
items.description: When Akamai announced its LayerX acquisition, the company joined a growing list of vendors adding secure enterprise browsers to their product portfolios.

items.title: Former US execs plead guilty to aiding tech support scammers
items.description: Two former executives of a call-tracking and analytics company pleaded guilty to concealing a years-long tech support fraud scheme that victimized individuals worldwide. [...]

items.title: Why the Supreme Court's Chatrie case could change the meaning of privacy in America
items.description: Lawyer Adam Unikowsky spoke with Recorded Future News about why he believes geofence searches are problematic and why the way the court rules could have a dramatic impact on Americans’ right to privacy.

items.title: Canadian man arrested, charged for running KimWolf DDos botnet
items.description: In court documents unsealed on Thursday, the Justice Department said Jacob Butler ran KimWolf as a DDoS-for-hire service that infected over a million devices worldwide.

items.title: In Other News: Industrial Router Exploitation, CISA KEV Nomination Form, Gas Station Hacking
items.description: Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: CISA contractor exposes credentials, Mythos testing and new features, Huawei router flaw triggered telecom blackout. The post In Other News: Industrial Router Exploitation, CISA KEV Nomination Form, Gas Station Hacking appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Trend Micro warns of Apex One zero-day exploited in the wild
items.description: Japanese cybersecurity software company Trend Micro has addressed an Apex One zero-day vulnerability exploited in attacks targeting Windows systems. [...]

items.title: Verizon DBIR: Healthcare Fends Off Increased Social Engineering Attacks
items.description: Ransomware and vendor breaches persist. The "2026 Data Breach Investigations Report" (DBIR) highlights how evolving social engineering tactics make the sector more vulnerable.

items.title: Drupal: Critical SQL injection flaw now targeted in attacks
items.description: Drupal is warning that hackers are attempting to exploit a "highly critical" SQL injection vulnerability announced earlier this week. [...]

items.title: Why Chargebacks are Just One Piece of the Fraud Puzzle
items.description: Fraud losses don't stop at chargebacks. False declines, account takeovers, and abuse also damage revenue and trust. IPQS breaks down why fraud teams need broader visibility into risk and customer impact. [...]

items.title: Canadian Man Arrested for Operating Kimwolf Botnet
items.description: Jacob Butler, 23, has been arrested in Canada and US authorities are seeking his extradition on computer hacking charges. The post Canadian Man Arrested for Operating Kimwolf Botnet appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Ubiquiti patches three max severity UniFi OS vulnerabilities
items.description: Ubiquiti has released security updates to patch three maximum severity vulnerabilities in UniFi OS that can be exploited by remote attackers without privileges. [...]

items.title: Megalodon GitHub Attack Targets 5,561 Repos with Malicious CI/CD Workflows
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new automated campaign called Megalodon that has pushed 5,718 malicious commits to 5,561 GitHub repositories within a six-hour window. "Using throwaway accounts and forged author identities (build-bot, auto-ci, ci-bot, pipeline-bot), the attacker injected GitHub Actions workflows containing base64-encoded bash payloads that exfiltrate CI

items.title: Making Vulnerable Drivers Exploitable Without Hardware - The BYOVD Perspective
items.description: 1 Introduction This article provides a technical analysis of how many Windows kernel mode drivers can be interacted with from user mode without the hardware they were developed for. This work was motivated by driver-oriented vulnerability research and the need to evaluate the exploitability of individual findings, which frequently affect code whose reachability is hardware-gated. The

items.title: ‘First VPN’ Cybercrime Service Disrupted, Administrator Arrested
items.description: The FBI says First VPN has been used by dozens of ransomware groups for network reconnaissance and intrusions. The post ‘First VPN’ Cybercrime Service Disrupted, Administrator Arrested appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: US and Canada arrest and charge suspected Kimwolf botnet admin
items.description: U.S. and Canadian authorities arrested and charged a Canadian man with operating the KimWolf distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) botnet, which infected nearly two million devices worldwide. [...]

items.title: Kimwolf DDoS Botnet Operator Arrested in Canada Over DDoS-for-Hire Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Thursday announced the arrest of a Canadian man in connection with allegedly operating a distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) botnet known as Kimwolf. In tandem, Jacob Butler (aka Dort), 23, Ottawa, Canada, has been charged with offenses related to the development and operation of the botnet. Kimwolf is assessed to be a variant of AISURU that specifically

items.title: TrendAI Patches Apex One Zero-Day Exploited in the Wild
items.description: CVE-2026-34926 is a directory traversal flaw that can be exploited against the on-premise version of Apex One. The post TrendAI Patches Apex One Zero-Day Exploited in the Wild appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Grafana Says Codebase and Other Data Stolen via TanStack Supply Chain Attack
items.description: Hackers accessed Grafana’s GitHub repositories after a token compromised in the TanStack attack was not rotated. The post Grafana Says Codebase and Other Data Stolen via TanStack Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: China's Webworm Uses Discord, Microsoft Graphs to Hack EU Governments
items.description: The advanced persistent threat group also relied on SOCKS proxies like SoftEther VPN, tunneling tools that act as a middleman between victim and attacker.

items.title: Cross-Platform NPM Stealer, (Fri, May 22nd)
items.description: I found a Node.js stealer that looked pretty well obfuscated. The file was not running out-of-the-box because it was uploaded on VT as â€œextracted-decoded.jsâ€ (and reformated). The SHA256 is 049300aa5dd774d6c984779a0570f59610399c71864b5d5c2605906db46ddeb9[1]. It did not run properly in a sandbox so only a static analysis was performed.

items.title: CISA Adds Exploited Langflow and Trend Micro Apex One Vulnerabilities to KEV
items.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added two security flaws impacting Langflow and Trend Micro Apex One to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation. The vulnerabilities in question are listed below - CVE-2025-34291 (CVSS score: 9.4) - An origin validation error vulnerability in Langflow that could

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Friday, May 22nd, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9942, (Fri, May 22nd)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
items.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.

items.title: Google API Keys Remain Active After Deletion
items.description: A security researcher discovered the API keys can still be used for up to 23 minutes after deletion, even though the cloud provider claims deletion is immediate.

items.title: Google accidentally exposed details of unfixed Chromium flaw
items.description: Google has accidentally leaked details about an unfixed issue in Chromium that keeps JavaScript running in the background even when the browser is closed, allowing remote code execution on the device. [...]

items.title: AI Agents Are Shifting Identity Security Budget Dynamics
items.description: AI agent projects are proliferating throughout the enterprise, and those AI agent identities require management, security, and governance. New Omdia research shows the AI agent identity budget dynamics are very different than traditional IAM projects.

items.title: Apple blocked over $11 billion in App Store fraud in 6 years
items.description: Apple revealed that it blocked over $11 billion in fraudulent App Store transactions over the last six years, more than $2.2 billion in potentially fraudulent App Store transactions in 2025 alone. [...]

items.title: Inside a Crypto Drainer: How to Spot it Before it Empties Your Wallet
items.description: Modern crypto drainers don't hack wallets. They trick users into approving malicious transactions. Flare explores how the Lucifer DaaS platform scales wallet theft through phishing and automation. [...]

items.title: Chinese APTs Share Linux Backdoor in Central Asia Telco Attacks
items.description: "Showboat" doesn't show off, but clearly it doesn't need to, as it's long helped China spy on small market communications providers.

items.title: Selective HTTP Proxying in Linux, (Thu, May 21st)
items.description: Recently, Rob wrote about a tool, Proxifier, that can intercept requests from specific processes. Proxifier is available for Windows, macOS, and Android. But I have not seen a generic Linux option yet. The advantage of a tool like Proxifier is the ability to target specific software. For debugging, reverse engineering, and similar tasks, selecting a specific process is quite useful, as it creates less noise to sift through and simplifies analysis.

items.title: Content Delivery Exploit Opens Websites to Brand Hijacking
items.description: The Underminr domain-fronting attack allows threat actors to modify Web requests and leverage trusted websites to cloak malicious activity.

items.title: Cisco Patches Critical Vulnerability in Secure Workload
items.description: Insufficient validation and authentication in the Secure Workload’s REST APIs provide remote attackers with Site Admin privileges. The post Cisco Patches Critical Vulnerability in Secure Workload appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA Enhances Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog to Include New Nomination Form
Ocean Emerges From Stealth With $28M for Agentic Email Security Platform
items.description: The company has developed a platform that uses specialized AI agents to inspect every incoming message. The post Ocean Emerges From Stealth With $28M for Agentic Email Security Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Apple Rejected 2 Million App Store Submissions in 2025 for Security and Fraud Prevention
items.description: The company blocked over 1.1 billion accounts and $2.2 billion in potentially fraudulent transactions. The post Apple Rejected 2 Million App Store Submissions in 2025 for Security and Fraud Prevention appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Cyber Pros Can't Decide If AI Is a Good or a Bad Thing
items.description: There is nothing cybersecurity professionals are more excited about, and nothing they fear more, than AI.

items.title: GitHub Confirms Breach, 4K Internal Repos Stolen
items.description: GitHub confirmed a data breach this week involving the theft of thousands of developer code repositories. One threat actor — TeamPCP — took credit.

items.title: Fake Android Apps Commit Carrier Billing Fraud for Premium Services
items.description: The disguised apps use WebView automation, JavaScript injection, and OTP interception to avoid detection and complete fraudulent subscriptions.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Wednesday, May 20th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9938, (Wed, May 20th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, May 19th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9936, (Tue, May 19th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Activity Through 2026-05-17, (Mon, May 18th)
items.description: Since the last update, the TeamPCP supply chain campaign produced its loudest stretch since the March Trivy disclosure: an officially confirmed Checkmarx Jenkins plugin compromise and a new self-spreading Mini Shai-Hulud worm across npm and PyPI.

items.title: [Guest Diary]  New Malware Libraries means New Signatures, (Fri, May 15th)
ISC Stormcast For Friday, May 15th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9934, (Fri, May 15th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
CISA’s 2025 Year in Review: Driving Security and Resilience Across Critical Infrastructure
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
